free_text: Production of 229Th for medical applications:
Excitation functions of low-energy protons on 232Th
targets
Contains Ni, Cu and Th results.
Final 232Th target results.
The On-Line Test Facility, a low-
intensity isotope separation on-line facility
primarily used for testing of ion sources and targets
for the production of radioactive ion beams,
Holifield Radioactive Ion Beam Facility.
Gamma-ray spectrometry was performed using a
50 cm3 high purity Ge detector.
Compiled by S.H.
BP: Updated authors, final 232Th results
and added (p,n) and (p,5n) reaction data.
Stack of thin Ni foils (2.24 mg/cm2 'sandwiched' by
thin Al foils (8.1 mg/cm2) held together with
epoxy glue at the edges to prevent release of
nuclides.
Total errors were obtained as the square root
of the sum of squares of the errors mentioned above
and ranged between 7 and 13%.

Data presented in Fig. 7 of the S,AIP-
1525,520,2013, sent by author (C.U.J.).
For the thin targets, the activity of
229Pa was measured using the 117.2 and 119.0 keV
gamma rays post chemical separation of the
protactinium from fission products and other actinides.
Table II, page 044607-5 of the final 232Th Ref.
Targets consisted of four assemblies of stacked
foils. The first two assemblies were considered the
thin targets (0.0125 mm thick). The first assembly
consisted of eight natural thorium foils (18.5-21.6 mg
cm-2), and the second assembly consisted of five
foils. Each of the thorium foils was mounted on a
thin (0.025 mm) high-purity aluminum foil disk. The
thorium foils were 1.25 x 1.25 cm squares, whereas
the aluminum foil disks had a diameter of 4 cm.
Total errors were obtained as the square root
of the sum of squares of the errors mentioned above
and ranged between 7 and 13%.
systematic uncertainty in gamma ray assay
systematic uncertainty in detector
efficiency
systematic uncertainty in proton
beam current
For the cross sections from Th targets,
an error for the chemical separation yield.
BP: Updated data using the final, rounded
numbers and sample information.
Direct analysis of the thorium foils after
irradiation.
